page: Microsoft Azure D48_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with an x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz and includes 48 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results place the D48_v5 in the average performance tier for most compute and memory operations. The instance achieved a single-core stress-ng score of 1899.19 and a multi-core score of 46526.02. Memory performance is highlighted by a strong PassMark memory score of 2788.8, with uncached large-block read bandwidth reaching 150.16 GB/sec. Cryptographic and image processing capabilities are notable, with the PassMark CPU encryption test scoring 42240.46 and Geekbench image processing scoring 14757.0, both placing in the top 25% of tested configurations.

The lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on external storage solutions, which is a key architectural tradeoff. While database operations perform strongly at 21553.04 operations in PassMark, large-scale LLM text generation is a bottleneck; the 70B parameter model text generation speed is weak at 2.62 tokens per second. Consequently, this instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, software compilation, and database operations that do not require local high-speed scratch disks or GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure D48_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs, 24 physical cores, and 192.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities across standard compute, memory, and compression tasks, with strong results in CPU encryption, image processing, and database operations. However, text generation for large language models is a notable bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25% for 70B parameter models. This instance is suited for general-purpose enterprise applications, web serving, and database workloads that rely on external storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8370C processor, 192 GB of RAM, and no local storage, delivering balanced compute and memory performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, optimized for balanced enterprise workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring forty-eight virtual CPUs, robust memory bandwidth, and strong cryptographic performance without local storage.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores
192 GB of system memory providing 4 GB per vCPU
Strong performance in cryptographic operations and image processing
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for general-purpose enterprise applications and web serving
